squire.book.title: The Squire's Manual
squire.book.landing: Herein lies the collected wisdom of the Order of the Crest — a training manual for those who would bind a squire to their service. Whether you seek a steadfast companion for the deep mines, a tireless hand in the fields, or a sword-arm at your side in battle, these pages shall guide you. Read well, for the bond between knight and squire is forged in understanding, not mere command.
squire.category.the_bond.desc: On the summoning and command of a loyal squire — the sacred bond between master and servant.
squire.category.the_trades.desc: The working arts: mining, farming, fishing, and the disciplines of labour that keep a hold prosperous.
squire.category.the_blade.desc: On the arts of war — combat training, guard duty, and the mounted charge.
squire.category.the_ledger.desc: A complete register of orders your squire understands, written in the common tongue of command.
squire.entry.summoning.p1: Every great knight began as someone's squire. To summon yours, you must first craft a $(item)Squire's Crest$() — the sigil that binds servant to master.$(br2)Hold the Crest in hand and right-click. Your squire shall materialize before you, wreathed in soul-fire, ready for duty. A squire without a crest is a soldier without a banner — lost and purposeless.$(br2)Your squire begins life as a humble Servant, green and untested. Through experience — in labour and in battle — they shall rise through the ranks: Apprentice, Journeyman, Veteran, and in time, Champion.
squire.entry.summoning.p2: The Crest is your instrument of authority. Craft it and keep it close.$(br2)Should you wish to dismiss your squire, use $(thing)/squire recall$(). They shall return to the Crest, carrying their experience and inventory with them, ready to be called forth again when needed.$(br2)One knight, one squire. The Crest enforces this ancient law across all realms.
squire.entry.the_crest.p1: The Crest serves two purposes: it is both the instrument of summoning and the tool of dominion over land.$(br2)When your squire is active, right-click any block with the Crest to mark the first corner of a work area. Right-click a second block to complete the selection. You will see the dimensions reported to you.$(br2)This selected region becomes your squire's domain — the ground they will mine, the soil they will farm.
squire.entry.the_crest.p2: $(l)Clearing a Selection$()$(br)Use $(thing)/squire clear$() while holding the Crest to wipe the current selection.$(br2)$(l)Area Commands$()$(br)Once an area is marked, issue $(thing)/squire mine$() or $(thing)/squire farm$() to put your squire to work within the bounds.$(br2)A well-marked field is half the harvest. Take care with your corners — too vast an area, and your squire shall labour long before returning with spoils.
squire.entry.modes.p1: A squire must know when to follow, when to hold, and when to fight. Three modes govern their bearing:$(br2)$(l)Follow$() — The default stance. Your squire walks at your heel, a shadow in leather. They will follow you through any terrain and across dimensions.$(br2)$(l)Sit$() — Command your squire to hold position. They will remain where they stand, patient as a sentinel, until ordered otherwise. Useful when you need them to wait at camp or guard a passage.
squire.entry.modes.p2: $(l)Guard$() — The battle-ready stance. Your squire claims their ground and attacks any hostile creature that dares approach. Set them at a chokepoint or at your gate, and they will hold it.$(br2)$(l)Setting Modes$()$(br)Press $(thing)R$() to open the radial command menu and select the desired mode. Alternatively, use the command $(thing)/squire mode follow|sit|guard$().$(br2)A squire in the wrong mode is a liability. Know the ground, and set the stance to match it.
squire.entry.roles_overview.p1: Beyond following and fighting, a squire may be assigned a trade — a persistent vocation that keeps them occupied while you attend to greater matters.$(br2)Roles are set with $(thing)/squire role miner|farmer|fisher|none$(). Once assigned, a squire works continuously at their trade: mining ore, tending crops, or hauling fish from the river. They do not stop until told.$(br2)This stands apart from the task queue, which handles one-off orders. A role is a calling; a task is an errand.
squire.entry.roles_overview.p2: $(l)The Task Queue$()$(br)For single assignments, use $(thing)/squire queue mine|farm|fish|clear$(). Tasks in the queue are completed one at a time.$(br2)$(l)Choosing Between Role and Queue$()$(br)If you need ongoing labour — a mine that never sleeps, fields forever tended — assign a role. If you need a one-time harvest or a single shaft dug, queue the task.$(br2)A knight who knows the difference between a standing order and a dispatch runs a tighter hold.
squire.entry.mining.p1: The art of mining is not brute force but measured purpose. Your squire can mine in two ways: area mining and shaft mining.$(br2)$(l)Area Mining$()$(br)Select a region with the Crest (right-click two corners), then issue $(thing)/squire mine$(). Your squire will clear every block within the marked bounds in a clean sweep pattern, working layer by layer from top to bottom.
squire.entry.mining.p2: $(l)Shaft Mining$()$(br)For vertical operations, use $(thing)/squire shaft [depth]$() to bore a 1x1 shaft downward, or $(thing)/squire shaft 2x2 [depth]$() for a wider bore. The depth argument controls how far they dig (1 to 128 blocks).$(br2)Your squire will place torches in dark areas as they go — a sapper's instinct that keeps the work safe from ambush.
squire.entry.mining.p3: $(l)Tips for the Commander$()$(br)• Ensure your squire carries a pickaxe, or has materials to craft one$(br)• They will deposit mined goods in the supply chest when their packs are 80% full$(br)• For continuous mining, assign the $(thing)miner$() role rather than queuing individual tasks$(br)• Area mining respects the Crest selection — mark your claim carefully
squire.entry.farming.p1: A well-fed army marches on a farmer's back. Your squire can tend fields with the same discipline they bring to the blade.$(br2)Select an area with the Crest, then issue $(thing)/squire farm$(). Your squire will harvest ripe crops and replant them in one smooth pass. Between harvests, they patrol the rows, waiting for the next crop to ripen.$(br2)Without a Crest selection, $(thing)/squire farm$() works a 16x16 area around the squire's current position.
squire.entry.farming.p2: $(l)Requirements$()$(br)• Your squire needs a $(item)hoe$() in their inventory$(br)• They must carry seeds or have access to the supply chest for replanting$(br2)$(l)The Farmer's Role$()$(br)Assign $(thing)/squire role farmer$() for continuous farming. Your squire will loop their patrol endlessly, harvesting each crop the moment it ripens.$(br2)The fields do not rest, and neither does a squire with purpose.
squire.entry.fishing.p1: Not every battle is won with steel. Sometimes the greatest service a squire can render is a full larder.$(br2)Issue $(thing)/squire fish$() and your squire will seek the nearest body of water, stand upon the shore, and cast their line. They work without complaint until recalled or reassigned.$(br2)$(l)Requirements$()$(br)• Your squire needs a $(item)fishing rod$() in their inventory$(br)• They will find water on their own — no need to lead them to it$(br2)For continuous fishing, assign $(thing)/squire role fisher$(). A patient squire with a rod can feed a fortress.
squire.entry.home_chest.p1: When provisions run low — or packs run heavy — a trained squire knows to seek the supply chest without being told.$(br2)To designate a supply chest, look directly at any chest and use $(thing)/squire homechest$(). Your squire will remember this location and return to it whenever their inventory reaches 80% capacity.
squire.entry.home_chest.p2: After depositing their haul, the squire returns to their previous task — whether mining, farming, or fishing — without needing a new order.$(br2)$(l)Clearing the Chest$()$(br)Use $(thing)/squire homechest clear$() to remove the designation. Your squire will carry their burden until you assign a new one.$(br2)A supply line is the backbone of any campaign. Set the chest before the work begins, not after your squire is drowning in cobblestone.
squire.entry.tool_crafting.p1: A resourceful squire does not stand idle when their pickaxe shatters. Given raw materials — planks and sticks — they will fashion a replacement tool from what they carry.$(br2)Your squire always crafts the cheapest tier available. They are practical, not proud. Give them oak planks and sticks, and they will produce a wooden pickaxe. Give them cobblestone, and stone tools follow.
squire.entry.tool_crafting.p2: $(l)Configuration$()$(br)The maximum tier your squire will craft is controlled in the mod's configuration (default: stone). This prevents a squire from burning through your diamond supply on disposable tools.$(br2)$(l)Best Practice$()$(br)Keep a stack of planks and sticks in the supply chest. Your squire will resupply and craft as needed, maintaining an unbroken work cycle.$(br2)A squire who can mend their own kit is worth two who cannot.
squire.entry.combat_basics.p1: Steel is the final argument. When words and walls fail, your squire must be ready.$(br2)A squire will engage any hostile creature that comes within striking distance. They do not need orders to draw their blade — the instinct to defend is trained into them from the first summon.
squire.entry.combat_basics.p2: $(l)Equipping for Battle$()$(br)Arm your squire well. Place a sword in their hand and armour on their back through their inventory (sneak + right-click the squire).$(br2)$(l)Progression$()$(br)Every kill earns experience. As your squire levels, their combat abilities sharpen — faster strikes, better damage, and greater resilience. A Champion-rank squire is a force few mobs can withstand.$(br2)$(l)The Squire's Kit$()$(br)The $(item)Squire Halberd$() and $(item)Squire Shield$() are purpose-built for your companion. Equip them for the best results.
squire.entry.guard_mode.p1: There are times when a knight must ride out, leaving their holdings defended. Guard mode turns your squire into a stationary sentinel.$(br2)Set guard mode via the radial menu ($(thing)R$() key) or with $(thing)/squire mode guard$(). Your squire will hold their position and attack any hostile creature that enters their patrol radius.
squire.entry.guard_mode.p2: $(l)Tactical Use$()$(br)• Station guards at chokepoints, bridges, and doorways$(br)• Pair with a supply chest nearby so they can resupply$(br)• A well-armoured squire in guard mode can hold a corridor indefinitely$(br2)$(l)Patrol$()$(br)Use $(thing)/squire patrol$() to have your squire walk a patrol route near their guard position. Use $(thing)/squire patrol stop$() to end the patrol.$(br2)A fortress without a guard is a house with an open door.
squire.entry.mounted_combat.p1: A squire on horseback is worth three on foot. The mounted charge has broken more battle lines than any siege engine.$(br2)To mount your squire, lead a saddled horse near them and issue $(thing)/squire mount$(). Your squire will vault into the saddle and gain the mobility of a cavalier.
squire.entry.mounted_combat.p2: $(l)The Lance$()$(br)The $(item)Squire Halberd$() gains extended reach while the squire is mounted, striking enemies before they can close to melee range.$(br2)$(l)Dismounting$()$(br)Use $(thing)/squire dismount$() to return your squire to foot.$(br2)$(l)Tips$()$(br)• The horse must be saddled before the squire can mount$(br)• Mounted squires are faster but cannot mine or farm$(br)• Guard mode + mounted = mobile patrol with devastating charge attacks
squire.entry.command_reference.p1: $(l)Summoning & Control$()$(br)$(thing)/squire recall$() — Dismiss squire to the Crest$(br)$(thing)/squire mode <follow|sit|guard>$() — Set behaviour mode$(br)$(thing)/squire info$() — Display squire status and stats$(br)$(thing)/squire list$() — List all active squires (server)$(br)$(thing)/squire name <text>$() — Rename your squire$(br)$(thing)/squire appearance <variant>$() — Set appearance (male/female)$(br)$(thing)/squire kill$() — Permanently destroy a squire (admin)
squire.entry.command_reference.p2: $(l)Roles (Persistent)$()$(br)$(thing)/squire role <miner|farmer|fisher|none>$() — Assign trade$(br2)$(l)Tasks (One-shot)$()$(br)$(thing)/squire mine$() — Mine selected area$(br)$(thing)/squire shaft [depth]$() — Dig 1x1 shaft$(br)$(thing)/squire shaft 2x2 [depth]$() — Dig 2x2 shaft$(br)$(thing)/squire farm$() — Farm selected area$(br)$(thing)/squire fish$() — Fish nearest water$(br)$(thing)/squire clear$() — Clear Crest area selection$(br)$(thing)/squire place <pos>$() — Place block at position
squire.entry.command_reference.p3: $(l)Logistics$()$(br)$(thing)/squire homechest$() — Set supply chest (look at chest)$(br)$(thing)/squire homechest clear$() — Remove supply chest$(br)$(thing)/squire store [pos]$() — Store items in chest$(br)$(thing)/squire fetch [pos]$() — Fetch items from chest$(br2)$(l)Combat$()$(br)$(thing)/squire mount$() — Mount nearby saddled horse$(br)$(thing)/squire dismount$() — Dismount from horse$(br)$(thing)/squire patrol$() — Begin patrol route$(br)$(thing)/squire patrol stop$() — End patrol$(br2)$(l)Task Queue$()$(br)$(thing)/squire queue <mine|farm|fish|clear>$() — Manage task queue$(br2)$(l)Debug$()$(br)$(thing)/squire godmode$() — Toggle invulnerability (admin)
